title: juncus graecus
intro: Juncus graecus, also known as Greek rush or rush, is a perennial plant species belonging to the genus Juncus, commonly found in wetlands and grasslands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits also include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The herbal preparations of Juncus graecus often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or taken sublingually to harness its medicinal properties.
